title: An exception object should avoid having pointer type
description: An exception object should not have pointer typeAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugmisra-c++2008Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoWhen an exception is a pointer, it is difficult for the code that catches the exception to determine whether or not it needs to delete the pointed-to object. It is even more complicated than a traditional manual memory management scenario because thethrowand the correspondingcatchcan be far apart.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
int* errorCode = new int(42);
throw errorCode; // Noncompliant: throwing a pointer
throw new std::runtime_error("Error"); // Noncompliant: throwing a pointer to exception
compliant:
int errorCode = 42;
throw errorCode; // Compliant: throwing a value, not a pointer
throw std::runtime_error("Error"); // Compliant: throwing exception object by value

checks.message: Avoid throwing pointers - throw exception objects by value instead
